rulings.Case Title: STEPHEN SCHNEIDER VS. COCOA RESIDENTIAL OWNERS' ASSOCIATION, INC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, December 27, 2022, line 1. Cross-Defendant's Motion to Strike is continued to February 15, 2023. No later than January 19, 2023 Cross-Complaint to file a supplemental brief limited to the following: (1) clearly identify for each cause of action all allegations in the Cross-Complaint (by paragraph, page and line number) that are asserted merely for back ground information; (2) clearly identify (by paragraph, page and line number) for each cause of action factual assertions in paragraphs 15, 17-25 (See Opposition 9:5-8) supporting the claim(s) asserted in each cause of action. No argument shall be included in the supplemental brief. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: LSL PROPERTIES B14 DE,LLC VS. ALDO CARIC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: HEARING ON CLAIM OF RIGHT TO POSSESSION
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, December 27, 2022, line 3. Anastasia Meade's Claim of Right to Possession. Hearing Required. By no later than 9:00 a.m. on December 27, 2022 Anastasia Mead to present her evidence supporting claim of right to possession to the Court and opposing counsel (e.g. Driving License reflecting the property address before May 26, 2022; rent receipts; utility bills; communications with the owner of the property, etc). Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: SHUXIAN FENG VS. JOSE LOPEZ ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, December 27, 2022, line 5. Defendant's Demurrer to Amended Complaint is OVERRULED, 5 days (from proper verification being filed and served) to answer. Plaintiff sufficiently alleges that payment was made. Any factual issues regarding this "payment" being valid (e.g. negotiable; sufficient, etc) cannot be addressed on a demurrer. The burden is on the Plaintiff to prove the payment at a later stage of the litigation. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: SHUXIAN FENG VS. JOSE LOPEZ ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, December 27, 2022, line 5. Defendant's Motion to Strike Amended Complaint is GRANTED. Motion to Strike was previously granted on the grounds listed in the Notice to Motion filed on September 14, 2022. Plaintiff is ordered to comply with this Court's Ocober 24, 2022 ruling. Plaintiff shall have 5 days from notice of entry of order to file a complaint verification addressing the issues raised by the Defendant.
